dsl_rule.translation_notes: Dead-cat bounce pattern entry: SHORT when price closes below the Donchian lower band (formation low) AND closes lower than previous close, confirming the downward breakout. Used Donchian channels (dc_upper/dc_lower) to represent the formation boundaries. Exit when price closes above the Donchian upper band (reversal). The source text indicates a 3-week prior upward price movement (dead-cat bounce context) and horizontal consolidation, which would be reflected in the formation structure rather than as separate conditions. Success rates and volume data marked AUTHOR_SILENT per original signal.

source_rule_text: Dead-Cat Bounce: A breakout occurs when price closes beyond the formation high or low. A downward breakout is confirmed when price drops below the formation low and closes even lower.
